text: A thread that calls wait() on any object becomes inactive until another thread calls notify() on that object.

entity_types.class: A class defines the structure and behavior of objects, including fields, methods, and constructors, serving as a template for object instantiation.
entity_types.method: A method is a block of code that performs a specific task, defined within a class or interface, and can be invoked on objects or directly via the class (if static).
entity_types.interface: An interface defines a contract of methods that a class must implement. It can be functional or annotation-based.
entity_types.package: A package is a namespace that groups related classes and interfaces for better organization.

text: helper functions like filternotnull() which remove nulls from collections, or listofnotnull() for returning a zero or single item list from a possibly null value.

entity_types.class: A class defines the structure and behavior of objects, including fields, methods, and constructors, serving as a template for object instantiation. Special variants include exception and generic classes, with object singletons and library or package-based classes for specific usages.
entity_types.interface: An interface defines a contract of methods that a class must implement. It can represent standard, functional, or mapped variations for interoperability.
entity_types.property: Represents data members, which can include standard and instance properties, along with delegated properties that provide additional utility.
entity_types.package: A package is a namespace that groups related classes and interfaces for better organization and modularization. Libraries or packages can represent broader collections.
entity_types.function: A function defines a reusable block of code for a specific action, supporting forms such as suspending, lifecycle, intermediate, terminal, and higher-order functions alongside standard and built-script implementations.

text: When converting a string to and from a []byte or a []rune, Go does O(n) work even though conversions are "syntax".

entity_types.type: A named specification that defines the structure, behavior, and operations for values, including basic types, composite types, and custom type definitions.
entity_types.const: A named identifier that represents an immutable value determined at compile time, declared at package level or within a function scope.
entity_types.var: A named identifier that represents a mutable storage location for a value of a specific type, declared at package level or within a function scope.
entity_types.package: A collection of related Go source files that are compiled together as a unit, providing a namespace for constants, variables, functions, and types.
entity_types.method: A function associated with a specific type through a receiver, providing behavior that operates on instances of that type.
entity_types.func: A named block of code that performs a specific task, can accept parameters and return values, and may be declared at package level or as a method.

text: Plus it has a DataSetHelper that lets you use DataSets and DataTables to easily work with Excel data.

entity_types.class: A class defines the structure and behavior of objects, including fields, methods, and constructors, serving as a template for object instantiation.
entity_types.method: A method is a block of code that performs a specific task, defined within a class or interface, and can be invoked on objects or directly via the class (if static).
entity_types.interface: An interface defines a contract of methods that a class must implement. It can be functional or annotation-based.
entity_types.package: A package is a namespace that groups related classes and interfaces for better organization.
entity_types.property: A property is a member of a class or struct that provides a mechanism for reading, writing, or computing the value of a private field.
entity_types.enum: An enum is a distinct type with a set of named values representing constants.
entity_types.delegate: A delegate is a type that defines a method signature and can hold references to methods with the same signature.
entity_types.struct: A struct is a value type that defines a structure, often used to represent lightweight data structures.
entity_types.event: An event is a member that enables a class or object to provide notifications to other classes or objects when something of interest occurs.

text: json.dumps() converts a dictionary to str object, not a json object! So you have to load your str into a dict to use it by using json.loads() method.

entity_types.class: A class defines the structure and behavior of objects, including fields, methods, and constructors, serving as a template for object instantiation.
entity_types.method: A method is a block of code that performs a specific task, defined within a class or interface, and can be invoked on objects or directly via the class (if static).
entity_types.function: A function is a reusable block of code that performs a specific task, which can be built-in or user-defined.
entity_types.library: A library is a collection of modules or packages that provide reusable functionality for software development.
entity_types.property: A property is a member of a class or struct that provides a mechanism for reading, writing, or computing the value of a private field.
entity_types.interface: An interface defines a contract of methods that a class must implement, specifying behavior without providing implementation.

text: Every Iterator implements IntoIterator which performs an identity conversion (into_iter() just returns the iterator it is called on), so you can use Iterator instances in for loops as well.

entity_types.module: A namespace that contains definitions of other items (functions, structs, etc.), allowing for organization and visibility control (pub).
entity_types.struct: A composite data type that groups together variables (fields) under a single name.
entity_types.enum: A type that defines a set of named variants, each of which can hold data.
entity_types.function: A standalone block of code that takes parameters, performs operations, and optionally returns a value.
entity_types.method: A method is a function associated with a type, providing behavior that operates on instances of that type.
entity_types.trait: A trait defines a contract of methods that a type must implement, enabling polymorphism and abstraction.

output:
### entities ###
Iterator: trait; IntoIterator: trait; into_iter(): method
